# gerrit-cli

> CLI for Gerrit code review. Query changes, post comments, vote, manage reviewers, checkout/push code, check build status. Supports XML output for LLM pipelines.

## Installation

```bash
bun install -g @cloudglab/gerrit-cli
gerrit-cli setup  # Configure credentials interactively
```

## Change Identifiers

Commands accept change number (12345) or Change-ID (If5a3ae8...). Many commands auto-detect from HEAD commit when omitted.

## Commands

### View Changes

```bash
gerrit-cli show [change-id]              # Full change info: metadata, diff, comments
gerrit-cli show --xml                    # XML output for LLM consumption
gerrit-cli diff <change-id>              # Get diff
gerrit-cli diff <change-id> --files-only # List changed files only
gerrit-cli comments <change-id>          # View all comments with context
gerrit-cli search "owner:self status:open"  # Gerrit query syntax
gerrit-cli search "project:foo" -n 10    # Limit results
```

### List Changes

```bash
gerrit-cli mine                  # Your open changes
gerrit-cli incoming              # Changes awaiting your review
```

### Post Comments

```bash
gerrit-cli comment <change-id> -m "LGTM"                           # Overall comment
echo "Review text" | gerrit-cli comment <change-id>                # Piped input
gerrit-cli comment <change-id> --file src/foo.ts --line 42 -m "Fix this"  # Line comment
gerrit-cli comment <change-id> --file src/foo.ts --line 42 -m "Bug" --unresolved
```

Batch comments via JSON stdin:

```bash
echo '[
  {"file": "src/main.ts", "line": 10, "message": "Add type annotation"},
  {"file": "src/api.ts", "line": 25, "message": "Handle error", "unresolved": true}
]' | gerrit-cli comment <change-id> --batch
```

JSON schema: `{"file": string, "line": number, "message": string, "unresolved"?: boolean, "side"?: "PARENT"|"REVISION", "range"?: {start_line, end_line, start_character?, end_character?}}`

### Vote

```bash
gerrit-cli vote <change-id> --code-review +2
gerrit-cli vote <change-id> --code-review +2 --verified +1 -m "LGTM"
gerrit-cli vote <change-id> --label "Custom-Label" +1
```

### Manage Reviewers

```bash
gerrit-cli add-reviewer user@example.com -c <change-id>
gerrit-cli add-reviewer user1 user2 -c <change-id>
gerrit-cli add-reviewer --group team-name -c <change-id>
gerrit-cli add-reviewer --cc user@example.com -c <change-id>
gerrit-cli remove-reviewer user@example.com -c <change-id>
```

### Topic Management

```bash
gerrit-cli topic <change-id>              # View current topic
gerrit-cli topic <change-id> my-feature   # Set topic
gerrit-cli topic <change-id> --delete     # Remove topic
```

### Git Operations

```bash
gerrit-cli checkout <change-id>           # Fetch and checkout change
gerrit-cli checkout <change-id> --detach  # Detached HEAD
gerrit-cli push                           # Push for review
gerrit-cli push -r alice@example.com -t my-topic --wip
gerrit-cli push --dry-run                 # Preview push
```

### Change Lifecycle

```bash
gerrit-cli rebase [change-id]             # Rebase onto target branch
gerrit-cli submit <change-id>             # Submit for merge
gerrit-cli abandon <change-id> -m "reason"
gerrit-cli restore <change-id>
```

### Build Status

```bash
gerrit-cli build-status [change-id]                    # Check status: pending|running|success|failure
gerrit-cli build-status --watch                        # Poll until completion
gerrit-cli build-status --watch --exit-status && deploy.sh  # Exit 1 on failure
```

### Extract URLs

```bash
gerrit-cli extract-url "build-summary-report" | tail -1   # Latest Jenkins URL
gerrit-cli extract-url "jenkins" --json | jq -r '.urls[-1]'
```

### Groups

```bash
gerrit-cli groups                         # List groups
gerrit-cli groups --pattern "^team-.*"    # Filter by regex
gerrit-cli groups-show <group-id>         # Group details
gerrit-cli groups-members <group-id>      # List members
```

### Utilities

```bash
gerrit-cli status                         # Check connection
gerrit-cli open <change-id>               # Open in browser
gerrit-cli install-hook                   # Install commit-msg hook
gerrit-cli projects                       # List projects
```

## Output Formats

Most commands support `--xml` for structured LLM output:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<comment_result>
  <status>success</status>
  <change_id>12345</change_id>
</comment_result>
```

Some commands also support `--json`.

## Gerrit Query Syntax

Common operators for `gerrit-cli search`:

- `owner:USER` - Changes owned by user (use 'self' for yourself)
- `status:STATE` - open, merged, abandoned
- `project:NAME` - Filter by project
- `branch:NAME` - Filter by branch
- `reviewer:USER` - Changes where user is reviewer
- `is:wip` - Work-in-progress
- `is:submittable` - Ready to submit
- `age:TIME` - Time since update (1d, 2w, 1mon)
- `label:Code-Review+2` - Filter by vote

## Common Workflows

### Review a change

```bash
gerrit-cli show 12345                     # View change
gerrit-cli diff 12345                     # See code changes
gerrit-cli comment 12345 -m "LGTM"        # Add comment
gerrit-cli vote 12345 --code-review +2    # Approve
```

### Check build and get failures

```bash
gerrit-cli build-status --watch --exit-status
gerrit-cli extract-url "build-summary-report" | tail -1  # Get Jenkins URL
```

### Push with reviewers

```bash
gerrit-cli push -r alice@example.com -r bob@example.com -t feature-topic
```

## Exit Codes

- `0` - Success
- `1` - Error (or build failure with --exit-status)
- `2` - Timeout
- `3` - API/network error

## Configuration

Credentials stored in `~/.gerrit-cli/config.json`. Run `gerrit-cli setup` to configure:
- Gerrit host URL
- Username
- HTTP password (from Gerrit settings)

## Links

- [Gerrit Query Syntax](https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/user-search.html)
